 {"id":"bd-b80","title":"Repair: Implement scrub pipeline (detect corruption signals)","description":"Goal: detect corrupted blocks/metadata reliably.

Deliverables:
- Define signals:
  - ext4 metadata_csum / btrfs csum verification failures
  - structural invariant violations (bounds, tree ordering)
- Implement ScrubReport with per-block findings and severity.

Acceptance:
- Harness can inject corruption (flip bit) and scrub reports it deterministically.
- Scrub does not panic on corrupted data; it returns structured findings.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:24:28.702412790Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T01:27:16.268015147Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T01:27:16.267938143Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["repair"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-b80","depends_on_id":"bd-25k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:25:00.916224379Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-b80","depends_on_id":"bd-2vt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:25:00.995317684Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-ba1r","title":"Add btrfs COW left-borrow delete success coverage","description":"Fallback testing-conformance round: add positive btrfs COW delete-underflow coverage for the left-sibling borrow path, aligning success-path behavior with the existing allocator-failure left-borrow adversarial test. Update conformance/COVERAGE.md so the matrix explicitly tracks both left and right delete-borrow success coverage.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"EmeraldCitadel","created_at":"2026-04-18T13:14:49.696870787Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T13:25:39.576036945Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T13:25:39.573931440Z","close_reason":"Completed btrfs COW left-borrow delete success coverage; focused left-borrow test, workspace check, workspace clippy, fmt, diff check, and UBS critical scan passed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","testing"]}
 {"id":"bd-baxz9","title":"fuzz: add fuzz_detect_filesystem target","description":"ffs_core::detect_filesystem (crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:177) is the entry point for filesystem type detection from raw bytes. No fuzz coverage currently exists.

Create fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_detect_filesystem.rs with determinism checks.

Acceptance: fuzz target passes cargo fuzz build and exercises detect_filesystem without panics.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:34:05.561229075Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T22:59:29Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T22:59:29Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of implemented bead bd-kbe35; fuzz_detect_filesystem target now exists.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-bcmet","title":"Saturate block cache metrics counters","description":"`CacheMetrics` are documented as monotonic, but several `ArcState` counters still use wrapping `+=` arithmetic and `hit_ratio` adds hits + misses directly. Long-lived block caches can wrap counters or compute a zero denominator at numeric limits, misleading runtime telemetry. Route cache metric increments/additions through saturating arithmetic and add numeric-limit coverage.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod-fs","created_at":"2026-04-27T10:10:13Z","created_by":"cod-fs","updated_at":"2026-04-27T10:15:51Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T10:15:51Z","close_reason":"Routed ArcState cache metrics through saturating counter helpers, fixed hit_ratio denominator overflow at numeric limits, and added numeric-limit regressions for hit/miss/eviction/dirty-flush/ghost counters. Validation passed: focused ffs-block metric tests, fmt, diff-check, UBS touched file, workspace check, and workspace clippy.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-bbmh","title":"Docs: align shared fixture references with actual conformance fixture tree","description":"AGENTS.md currently says shared cross-component fixtures live under tests/fixtures, but the live conformance harness, CLI usage text, and integration tests use conformance/fixtures. Update the project instructions so the documented fixture paths match the real repo layout and harness commands. Acceptance: AGENTS.md fixture references point to conformance/fixtures where appropriate, with tests/fuzz corpus references left intact.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"FrostyCitadel","created_at":"2026-04-01T20:35:34.200068745Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-01T20:37:30.863053286Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T20:37:30.862783315Z","close_reason":"Aligned AGENTS.md fixture-path references with the actual split between conformance/fixtures and tests/fixtures","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","harness"]}
 {"id":"bd-bejk","title":"Fix dx_root test layout, read-directory EISDIR check, and close stale clippy bead","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-22T16:23:41.502809158Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-22T16:23:54.140304578Z","closed_at":"2026-03-22T16:23:54.139616074Z","close_reason":"Fixed 3 issues:
(1) ffs-core read() missing EISDIR check: reading a directory inode via FsOps::read() returned Ok with dir data instead of Err(EISDIR). Added is_dir() guard in ext4 read path. 471 ffs-core tests pass.
(2) ffs-ondisk parse_dx_root_basic test: dx_entry layout was wrong — test wrote explicit hash(4)+block(4) for entry 0, but dx_countlimit overlaps entry 0 (hash is implicit 0, only block at offset+4). Fixed test data layout.
(3) ffs-ondisk proptest dx_entries: same layout bug — restructured to match actual format with entry 0 block separate from subsequent hash+block pairs. 360 ffs-ondisk tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","correctness","ondisk"]}
 {"id":"bd-bivg2","title":"[FUSE] renameat2 RENAME_EXCHANGE + RENAME_NOREPLACE atomicity","description":"Wire RENAME_EXCHANGE and RENAME_NOREPLACE flags from FUSE FUSE_RENAME2 (opcode 45) through ffs-core::rename to the directory operations in ffs-dir.

Context:
- RENAME_NOREPLACE: rename fails with EEXIST if the target already exists, instead of overwriting it. Atomicity guarantee: the check + rename must be one critical section under the FuseInodeLocks parent guard so a concurrent create can't race.
- RENAME_EXCHANGE: atomically swap two existing entries (both must exist; either ENOENT). Must atomically update parent dir entries for both sides under a sorted parent-pair lock to avoid deadlock. Hash-tree (htree) directory updates need to be transactional within the journal.
- ext4 reference: fs/ext4/namei.c::ext4_rename2, ext4_rename_dir_prepare, ext4_rename_dir_finish. The exchange path swaps inode numbers in the dir entries while keeping all other fields.

Currently ffs-fuse's rename ignores the flags field on FUSE_RENAME2 — exchange degrades to dst-overwrite + src-creates loop which is non-atomic.

Acceptance:
- Test: spawn 2 threads; one does renameat2(A, B, RENAME_NOREPLACE) to existing target; the other does create(B). Either rename succeeds and create fails with EEXIST, or rename fails with EEXIST and create succeeds. Never: both succeed.
- Test: renameat2(X, Y, RENAME_EXCHANGE) where X is dir/x and Y is dir/y; verify post-condition lookup(dir, x).ino == old_y_ino && lookup(dir, y).ino == old_x_ino.
- xfstests generic/{375, 386, 446} (renameat2) pass on FUSE mount.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-25T08:27:42.013905221Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T08:43:15.122521264Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T08:43:15.120731997Z","close_reason":"RENAME_NOREPLACE shipped end-to-end via FsOps::rename2 trait method (default impl delegates to rename for flags=0, returns EINVAL otherwise) + OpenFs override that does atomic lookup-then-rename under the parent locks the dispatcher already holds. 6 new regressions cover: classic overwrite, NOREPLACE blocks existing target with EEXIST + both names survive, NOREPLACE with free target succeeds + inode preserved, EXCHANGE returns EINVAL, NOREPLACE|EXCHANGE returns EINVAL, unknown flag returns EINVAL. RENAME_EXCHANGE atomic dual-entry swap + RENAME_WHITEOUT char-device inode work deferred to bd-5lxot.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
